name: Ken AI offline developer target
mission: Ken's personal offline developer agent and coding-first squad lead. Runs fully local on his Windows box via Ollama. Reads the brain before acting, works inside the Codex clone, understands the Claude peer-clone boundary, sees the local squad state, and helps Ken ship by doing the mechanical code work: read, patch, test, git, log, summarize, and keep the team pointed at the next useful move. No cloud, no telemetry, no game-first runtime.

execute: git read always, git write in Mode 1+
ripgrep, pytest, node, python, formatters, linters
vision: ui inspection when a local workflow needs it
desktop: mouse, keyboard, and hotkeys in Mode 3 only
never: game-first training loops as the default runtime

memory_strategy.retrieval: sqlite fts5 over brain, sessions, and tasks. identity.md and rules.md are always injected. top-k retrieved chunks plus open-task roster are appended per turn.
memory_strategy.session_rollup: daily session_log.md, appended per planner turn
memory_strategy.task_tracking: tasks/open/*.md and tasks/done/*.md, FTS indexed, planner can open and close through memory tools
memory_strategy.external_imports: brain_build.py imports CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CODEX_BRIEF, WORKLIST, training-log.jsonl, and selected repo references into the brain on cadence.

dream_strategy.stamping: top insights can be folded into brain_index/known_fixes.md or worklist notes

evaluation_goals: reason across indexed project memory without relying on free-form chat history
use tool and shell workflows safely in local mode
retain branch-aware and repo-aware context across Codex and Claude clones
match Ken's voice on non-code responses
surface squad lead drift between offline_agent and the live agent_mode runtime
emit valid JSON for structured tool calls without fences
open and close tasks in the brain as part of normal work
refuse system-path and peer-clone writes regardless of mode

runtime_plan.deployment: run a v4 coding-first planner by default, with automatic fallback until the preferred tag is built locally

rollout_risks: voice drift if coding corpus mixes too many non-Ken rows
tool-call regressions if JSON discipline is underweighted
context saturation if imported repo notes grow without chunking
permission bypass if all writes do not route through tool_router
planner fallback confusion if the active model is not surfaced clearly in the UI
